NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. Set to “The Deer of the Dawn.” A Psalm of David. My God, My God, why have You forsaken Me? Why are You so far from helping Me, And from the words of My groaning?
NKJV.2: O My God, I cry in the daytime, but You do not hear; And in the night season, and am not silent.
NKJV.3: But You are holy, Enthroned in the praises of Israel.
NKJV.4: Our fathers trusted in You; They trusted, and You delivered them.
NKJV.5: They cried to You, and were delivered;  They trusted in You, and were not ashamed.
NKJV.6: But I am a worm, and no man; A reproach of men, and despised by the people.
NKJV.7: All those who see Me ridicule Me; They shoot out the lip, they shake the head, saying,
NKJV.8: “He  trusted in the LORD, let Him rescue Him;  Let Him deliver Him, since He delights in Him!”
NKJV.9: But You are He who took Me out of the womb; You made Me trust while on My mother’s breasts.
NKJV.10: I was cast upon You from birth. From My mother’s womb You have been My God.
NKJV.11: Be not far from Me, For trouble is near; For there is none to help.
NKJV.12: Many bulls have surrounded Me; Strong bulls of Bashan have encircled Me.
NKJV.13: They gape at Me with their mouths, Like a raging and roaring lion.
NKJV.14: I am poured out like water,  And all My bones are out of joint; My heart is like wax; It has melted within Me.
NKJV.15: My strength is dried up like a potsherd, And My tongue clings to My jaws; You have brought Me to the dust of death.
NKJV.16: For dogs have surrounded Me; The congregation of the wicked has enclosed Me.  They pierced My hands and My feet;
NKJV.17: I can count all My bones. They look and stare at Me.
NKJV.18: They divide My garments among them, And for My clothing they cast lots.
NKJV.19: But You, O LORD, do not be far from Me; O My Strength, hasten to help Me!
NKJV.20: Deliver Me from the sword, My precious life from the power of the dog.
NKJV.21: Save Me from the lion’s mouth And from the horns of the wild oxen!  You have answered Me.
NKJV.22: I will declare Your name to My brethren; In the midst of the assembly I will praise You.
NKJV.23: You who fear the LORD, praise Him! All you descendants of Jacob, glorify Him, And fear Him, all you offspring of Israel!
NKJV.24: For He has not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; Nor has He hidden His face from Him; But when He cried to Him, He heard.
NKJV.25: My praise shall be of You in the great assembly; I will pay My vows before those who fear Him.
NKJV.26: The poor shall eat and be satisfied; Those who seek Him will praise the LORD. Let your heart live forever!
NKJV.27: All the ends of the world Shall remember and turn to the LORD, And all the families of the nations Shall worship before You.
NKJV.28: For the kingdom is the LORD’s, And He rules over the nations.
NKJV.29: All the prosperous of the earth Shall eat and worship;  All those who go down to the dust Shall bow before Him, Even he who cannot keep himself alive.
NKJV.30: A posterity shall serve Him. It will be recounted of the Lord to the next generation,
NKJV.31: They will come and declare His righteousness to a people who will be born, That He has done this.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> upon Aijeleth<S>365</S> Shahar<S>7837</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. My God<S>410</S>, my God<S>410</S>, why hast thou forsaken<S>5800</S> me? why art thou so far<S>7350</S> from helping<S>3444</S> me, and from the words<S>1697</S> of my roaring<S>7581</S>? <sup>Aijeleth: or, the hind of the morning</sup> <sup>helping: Heb. my salvation</sup>
KJV.2: O my God<S>430</S>, I cry<S>7121</S> in the daytime<S>3119</S>, but thou hearest<S>6030</S> not; and in the night season<S>3915</S>, and am not silent<S>1747</S>. <sup>am: Heb. there is no silence to me</sup>
KJV.3: But thou art holy<S>6918</S>, O thou that inhabitest<S>3427</S> the praises<S>8416</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>.
KJV.4: Our fathers<S>1</S> trusted<S>982</S> in thee: they trusted<S>982</S>, and thou didst deliver<S>6403</S> them.
KJV.5: They cried<S>2199</S> unto thee, and were delivered<S>4422</S>: they trusted<S>982</S> in thee, and were not confounded<S>954</S>.
KJV.6: But I am a worm<S>8438</S>, and no man<S>376</S>; a reproach<S>2781</S> of men<S>120</S>, and despised<S>959</S> of the people<S>5971</S>.
KJV.7: All they that see<S>7200</S> me laugh me to scorn<S>3932</S>: they shoot out<S>6358</S> the lip<S>8193</S>, they shake<S>5128</S> the head<S>7218</S>, saying, <sup>shoot: Heb. open</sup>
KJV.8: He trusted<S>1556</S> on the LORD<S>3068</S> that he would deliver<S>6403</S> him: let him deliver<S>5337</S> him, seeing he delighted<S>2654</S> in him. <sup>He trusted: Heb. He rolled himself on</sup> <sup>seeing: or, if he delight in</sup>
KJV.9: But thou art he that took<S>1518</S> me out of the womb<S>990</S>: thou didst make me hope<S>982</S> when I was upon my mother's<S>517</S> breasts<S>7699</S>. <sup>didst: or, kept me in safety</sup>
KJV.10: I was cast<S>7993</S> upon thee from the womb<S>7358</S>: thou art my God<S>410</S> from my mother's<S>517</S> belly<S>990</S>.
KJV.11: Be not far<S>7368</S> from me; for trouble<S>6869</S> is near<S>7138</S>; for there is none to help<S>5826</S>. <sup>none: Heb. not a helper</sup>
KJV.12: Many<S>7227</S> bulls<S>6499</S> have compassed<S>5437</S> me: strong<S>47</S> bulls of Bashan<S>1316</S> have beset me round<S>3803</S>.
KJV.13: They gaped<S>6475</S> upon me with their mouths<S>6310</S>, as a ravening<S>2963</S> and a roaring<S>7580</S> lion<S>738</S>. <sup>gaped: Heb. opened their mouths against me</sup>
KJV.14: I am poured out<S>8210</S> like water<S>4325</S>, and all my bones<S>6106</S> are out of joint<S>6504</S>: my heart<S>3820</S> is like wax<S>1749</S>; it is melted<S>4549</S> in the midst<S>8432</S> of my bowels<S>4578</S>. <sup>out of: or, sundered</sup>
KJV.15: My strength<S>3581</S> is dried up<S>3001</S> like a potsherd<S>2789</S>; and my tongue<S>3956</S> cleaveth<S>1692</S> to my jaws<S>4455</S>; and thou hast brought<S>8239</S> me into the dust<S>6083</S> of death<S>4194</S>.
KJV.16: For dogs<S>3611</S> have compassed<S>5437</S> me: the assembly<S>5712</S> of the wicked<S>7489</S> have inclosed<S>5362</S> me: they pierced<S>3738</S> <S>738</S> my hands<S>3027</S> and my feet<S>7272</S>.
KJV.17: I may tell<S>5608</S> all my bones<S>6106</S>: they look<S>5027</S> and stare<S>7200</S> upon me.

KJV.20: Deliver<S>5337</S> my soul<S>5315</S> from the sword<S>2719</S>; my darling<S>3173</S> from the power<S>3027</S> of the dog<S>3611</S>. <sup>my darling: Heb. my only one</sup> <sup>power: Heb. hand</sup>
KJV.21: Save<S>3467</S> me from the lion's<S>738</S> mouth<S>6310</S>: for thou hast heard<S>6030</S> me from the horns<S>7161</S> of the unicorns<S>7214</S>.

KJV.24: For he hath not despised<S>959</S> nor abhorred<S>8262</S> the affliction<S>6039</S> of the afflicted<S>6041</S>; neither hath he hid<S>5641</S> his face<S>6440</S> from him; but when he cried<S>7768</S> unto him, he heard<S>8085</S>.

KJV.26: The meek<S>6035</S> shall eat<S>398</S> and be satisfied<S>7646</S>: they shall praise<S>1984</S> the LORD<S>3068</S> that seek<S>1875</S> him: your heart<S>3824</S> shall live<S>2421</S> for ever<S>5703</S>.
KJV.27: All the ends<S>657</S> of the world<S>776</S> shall remember<S>2142</S> and turn<S>7725</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>: and all the kindreds<S>4940</S> of the nations<S>1471</S> shall worship<S>7812</S> before<S>6440</S> thee.
KJV.28: For the kingdom<S>4410</S> is the LORD'S<S>3068</S>: and he is the governor<S>4910</S> among the nations<S>1471</S>.
KJV.29: All they that be fat<S>1879</S> upon earth<S>776</S> shall eat<S>398</S> and worship<S>7812</S>: all they that go down<S>3381</S> to the dust<S>6083</S> shall bow<S>3766</S> before<S>6440</S> him: and none can keep alive<S>2421</S> his own soul<S>5315</S>.
KJV.30: A seed<S>2233</S> shall serve<S>5647</S> him; it shall be accounted<S>5608</S> to the Lord<S>136</S> for a generation<S>1755</S>.

ESV.1: My God, my God, why have you forsaken me? Why are you so far from saving me, from the words of my groaning?
ESV.2: O my God, I cry by day, but you do not answer, and by night, but I find no rest.
ESV.3: Yet you are holy, enthroned on the praises  of Israel.
ESV.4: In you our fathers trusted; they trusted, and you delivered them.
ESV.5: To you they cried and were rescued; in you they trusted and were not put to shame.
ESV.6: But I am a worm and not a man, scorned by mankind and despised by the people.
ESV.7: All who see me mock me; they make mouths at me; they wag their heads;
ESV.8: “He trusts in the LORD; let him deliver him; let him rescue him, for he delights in him!”
ESV.9: Yet you are he who took me from the womb; you made me trust you at my mother's breasts.
ESV.10: On you was I cast from my birth, and from my mother's womb you have been my God.
ESV.11: Be not far from me, for trouble is near, and there is none to help.
ESV.12: Many bulls encompass me; strong bulls of Bashan surround me;
ESV.13: they open wide their mouths at me, like a ravening and roaring lion.
ESV.14: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint; my heart is like wax; it is melted within my breast;
ESV.15: my strength is dried up like a potsherd, and my tongue sticks to my jaws; you lay me in the dust of death.
ESV.16: For dogs encompass me; a company of evildoers encircles me; they have pierced my hands and feet  —
ESV.17: I can count all my bones — they stare and gloat over me;
ESV.18: they divide my garments among them, and for my clothing they cast lots.
ESV.19: But you, O LORD, do not be far off! O you my help, come quickly to my aid!
ESV.20: Deliver my soul from the sword, my precious life from the power of the dog!
ESV.21: Save me from the mouth of the lion! You have rescued  me from the horns of the wild oxen!
ESV.22: I will tell of your name to my brothers; in the midst of the congregation I will praise you:
ESV.23: You who fear the LORD, praise him! All you offspring of Jacob, glorify him, and stand in awe of him, all you offspring of Israel!
ESV.24: For he has not despised or abhorred the affliction of the afflicted, and he has not hidden his face from him, but has heard, when he cried to him.
ESV.25: From you comes my praise in the great congregation; my vows I will perform before those who fear him.
ESV.26: The afflicted  shall eat and be satisfied; those who seek him shall praise the LORD! May your hearts live forever!
ESV.27: All the ends of the earth shall remember and turn to the LORD, and all the families of the nations shall worship before you.
ESV.28: For kingship belongs to the LORD, and he rules over the nations.
ESV.29: All the prosperous of the earth eat and worship; before him shall bow all who go down to the dust, even the one who could not keep himself alive.
ESV.30: Posterity shall serve him; it shall be told of the Lord to the coming generation;
ESV.31: they shall come and proclaim his righteousness to a people yet unborn, that he has done it.

NASB.1: For the choir director; upon Aijeleth Hashshahar. A Psalm of David. My God, my God, why have You forsaken me? Far from my deliverance are the words of my groaning.
NASB.2: O my God, I cry by day, but You do not answer; And by night, but I have no rest.
NASB.3: Yet You are holy, O You who are enthroned upon the praises of Israel.
NASB.4: In You our fathers trusted; They trusted and You delivered them.
NASB.5: To You they cried out and were delivered; In You they trusted and were not disappointed.
NASB.6: But I am a worm and not a man, A reproach of men and despised by the people.
NASB.7: All who see me sneer at me; They separate with the lip, they wag the head, [saying],
NASB.8: 'Commit [yourself] to the LORD; let Him deliver him; Let Him rescue him, because He delights in him.'
NASB.9: Yet You are He who brought me forth from the womb; You made me trust [when] upon my mother's breasts.
NASB.10: Upon You I was cast from birth; You have been my God from my mother's womb.
NASB.11: Be not far from me, for trouble is near; For there is none to help.
NASB.12: Many bulls have surrounded me; Strong [bulls] of Bashan have encircled me.
NASB.13: They open wide their mouth at me, As a ravening and a roaring lion.
NASB.14: I am poured out like water, And all my bones are out of joint; My heart is like wax; It is melted within me.
NASB.15: My strength is dried up like a potsherd, And my tongue cleaves to my jaws; And You lay me in the dust of death.
NASB.16: For dogs have surrounded me; A band of evildoers has encompassed me; They pierced my hands and my feet.
NASB.17: I can count all my bones. They look, they stare at me;
NASB.18: They divide my garments among them, And for my clothing they cast lots.
NASB.19: But You, O LORD, be not far off; O You my help, hasten to my assistance.
NASB.20: Deliver my soul from the sword, My only [life] from the power of the dog.
NASB.21: Save me from the lion's mouth; From the horns of the wild oxen You answer me.
NASB.22: I will tell of Your name to my brethren; In the midst of the assembly I will praise You.
NASB.23: You who fear the LORD, praise Him; All you descendants of Jacob, glorify Him, And stand in awe of Him, all you descendants of Israel.
NASB.24: For He has not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; Nor has He hidden His face from him; But when he cried to Him for help, He heard.
NASB.25: From You [comes] my praise in the great assembly; I shall pay my vows before those who fear Him.
NASB.26: The afflicted will eat and be satisfied; Those who seek Him will praise the LORD. Let your heart live forever!
NASB.27: All the ends of the earth will remember and turn to the LORD, And all the families of the nations will worship before You.
NASB.28: For the kingdom is the LORD'S And He rules over the nations.
NASB.29: All the prosperous of the earth will eat and worship, All those who go down to the dust will bow before Him, Even he who cannot keep his soul alive.
NASB.30: Posterity will serve Him; It will be told of the Lord to the [coming] generation.
NASB.31: They will come and will declare His righteousness To a people who will be born, that He has performed [it].

NLT.1:
My God, my God, why have you abandoned me?
Why are you so far away when I groan for help?
NLT.2:
Every day I call to you, my God, but you do not answer.
Every night I lift my voice, but I find no relief.
NLT.3:
Yet you are holy,
enthroned on the praises of Israel.
NLT.4:
Our ancestors trusted in you,
and you rescued them.
NLT.5:
They cried out to you and were saved.
They trusted in you and were never disgraced.
NLT.6:
But I am a worm and not a man.
I am scorned and despised by all!
NLT.7:
Everyone who sees me mocks me.
They sneer and shake their heads, saying,
NLT.8:
“Is this the one who relies on the LORD?
Then let the LORD save him!
If the LORD loves him so much,
let the LORD rescue him!”
NLT.9:
Yet you brought me safely from my mother’s womb
and led me to trust you at my mother’s breast.
NLT.10:
I was thrust into your arms at my birth.
You have been my God from the moment I was born.
NLT.11:
Do not stay so far from me,
for trouble is near,
and no one else can help me.
NLT.12:
My enemies surround me like a herd of bulls;
fierce bulls of Bashan have hemmed me in!
NLT.13:
Like lions they open their jaws against me,
roaring and tearing into their prey.
NLT.14:
My life is poured out like water,
and all my bones are out of joint.
My heart is like wax,
melting within me.
NLT.15:
My strength has dried up like sunbaked clay.
My tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth.
You have laid me in the dust and left me for dead.
NLT.16:
My enemies surround me like a pack of dogs;
an evil gang closes in on me.
They have pierced my hands and feet.
NLT.17:
I can count all my bones.
My enemies stare at me and gloat.
NLT.18:
They divide my garments among themselves
and throw dice for my clothing.
NLT.19:
O LORD, do not stay far away!
You are my strength; come quickly to my aid!
NLT.20:
Save me from the sword;
spare my precious life from these dogs.
NLT.21:
Snatch me from the lion’s jaws
and from the horns of these wild oxen.
NLT.22:
I will proclaim your name to my brothers and sisters.
I will praise you among your assembled people.
NLT.23:
Praise the LORD, all you who fear him!
Honor him, all you descendants of Jacob!
Show him reverence, all you descendants of Israel!
NLT.24:
For he has not ignored or belittled the suffering of the needy.
He has not turned his back on them,
but has listened to their cries for help.
NLT.25:
I will praise you in the great assembly.
I will fulfill my vows in the presence of those who worship you.
NLT.26:
The poor will eat and be satisfied.
All who seek the LORD will praise him.
Their hearts will rejoice with everlasting joy.
NLT.27:
The whole earth will acknowledge the LORD and return to him.
All the families of the nations will bow down before him.
NLT.28:
For royal power belongs to the LORD.
He rules all the nations.
NLT.29:
Let the rich of the earth feast and worship.
Bow before him, all who are mortal,
all whose lives will end as dust.
NLT.30:
Our children will also serve him.
Future generations will hear about the wonders of the Lord.
NLT.31:
His righteous acts will be told to those not yet born.
They will hear about everything he has done.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician; set to “The Doe of the Morning.” A Psalm by David. My God, my God, why have you forsaken me? Why are you so far from helping me, and from the words of my groaning?
WEB.2: My God, I cry in the daytime, but you don’t answer; in the night season, and am not silent.
WEB.3: But you are holy, you who inhabit the praises of Israel.
WEB.4: Our fathers trusted in you. They trusted, and you delivered them.
WEB.5: They cried to you, and were delivered. They trusted in you, and were not disappointed.
WEB.6: But I am a worm, and no man; a reproach of men, and despised by the people.
WEB.7: All those who see me mock me. They insult me with their lips. They shake their heads, saying,
WEB.8: “He trusts in Yahweh; let him deliver him. Let him rescue him, since he delights in him.”
WEB.9: But you brought me out of the womb. You made me trust while at my mother’s breasts.
WEB.10: I was thrown on you from my mother’s womb. You are my God since my mother bore me.
WEB.11: Don’t be far from me, for trouble is near. For there is no one to help.
WEB.12: Many bulls have surrounded me. Strong bulls of Bashan have encircled me.
WEB.13: They open their mouths wide against me, lions tearing prey and roaring.
WEB.14: I am poured out like water. All my bones are out of joint. My heart is like wax; it is melted within me.
WEB.15: My strength is dried up like a potsherd. My tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth. You have brought me into the dust of death.
WEB.16: For dogs have surrounded me. A company of evildoers have enclosed me. They have pierced my hands and feet.
WEB.17: I can count all of my bones. They look and stare at me.
WEB.18: They divide my garments among them. They cast lots for my clothing.
WEB.19: But don’t be far off, Yahweh. You are my help: hurry to help me.
WEB.20: Deliver my soul from the sword, my precious life from the power of the dog.
WEB.21: Save me from the lion’s mouth! Yes, from the horns of the wild oxen, you have answered me.
WEB.22: I will declare your name to my brothers. Among the assembly, I will praise you.
WEB.23: You who fear Yahweh, praise him! All you descendants of Jacob, glorify him! Stand in awe of him, all you descendants of Israel!
WEB.24: For he has not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted, Neither has he hidden his face from him; but when he cried to him, he heard.
WEB.25: Of you comes my praise in the great assembly. I will pay my vows before those who fear him.
WEB.26: The humble shall eat and be satisfied. They shall praise Yahweh who seek after him. Let your hearts live forever.
WEB.27: All the ends of the earth shall remember and turn to Yahweh. All the relatives of the nations shall worship before you.
WEB.28: For the kingdom is Yahweh’s. He is the ruler over the nations.
WEB.29: All the rich ones of the earth shall eat and worship. All those who go down to the dust shall bow before him, even he who can’t keep his soul alive.
WEB.30: Posterity shall serve him. Future generations shall be told about the Lord.
WEB.31: They shall come and shall declare his righteousness to a people that shall be born, for he has done it.

CSB17.18:
They divided my garments among themselves,
and they cast lots for my clothing. ⓓ
CSB17.19:
But you, LORD, don’t be far away. ⓔ
My strength, come quickly to help me. ⓕ
CSB17.20:
Rescue my life from the sword, ⓖ
my only life [7] from the power of these dogs.
CSB17.21:
Save me from the lion’s mouth, ⓗ
from the horns of wild oxen.
You answered me! [8]
CSB17.22:
I will proclaim your name to my brothers and sisters;
I will praise you in the assembly. ⓘ
CSB17.23:
You who fear the LORD, praise him! ⓙ
All you descendants of Jacob, honor him!
All you descendants of Israel, revere him! ⓚ
CSB17.24:
For he has not despised or abhorred
the torment of the oppressed. ⓛ
He did not hide his face from him ⓜ
but listened when he cried to him for help. ⓝ
CSB17.25:
I will give praise in the great assembly
because of you; ⓐ
I will fulfill my vows
before those who fear you. [9] ⓑ
CSB17.26:
The humble will eat and be satisfied; ⓒ
those who seek the LORD will praise him.
May your hearts live forever! ⓓ
CSB17.27:
All the ends of the earth will remember
and turn to the LORD.
All the families of the nations
will bow down before you, ⓔ
CSB17.28:
for kingship belongs to the LORD;
he rules the nations. ⓕ
CSB17.29:
All who prosper on earth will eat and bow down;
all those who go down to the dust
will kneel before him —
even the one who cannot preserve his life. ⓖ
CSB17.30:
Their descendants will serve him;
the next generation will be told about the Lord. ⓗ
CSB17.31:
They will come and declare his righteousness;
to a people yet to be born
they will declare what he has done. ⓘ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; set to [the tune of] Aijeleth Hashshahar (The Doe of the Dawn). A Psalm of David. My God, my God, why have You forsaken me?
Why are You so far from helping me, and from the words of my groaning?
AMP.2:
O my God, I call out by day, but You do not answer;
And by night, but I find no rest nor quiet.
AMP.3:
But You are holy,
O You who are enthroned in [the holy place where] the praises of Israel [are offered].
AMP.4:
In You our fathers trusted [leaned on, relied on, and were confident];
They trusted and You rescued them.
AMP.5:
They cried out to You and were delivered;
They trusted in You and were not disappointed or ashamed.
AMP.6:
But I am [treated as] a worm [insignificant and powerless] and not a man;
I am the scorn of men and despised by the people.
AMP.7:
All who see me laugh at me and mock me;
They [insultingly] open their lips, they shake their head, saying,
AMP.8:
"He trusted and committed himself to the LORD, let Him save him.
Let Him rescue him, because He delights in him."
AMP.9:
Yet You are He who pulled me out of the womb;
You made me trust when on my mother's breasts.
AMP.10:
I was cast upon You from birth;
From my mother's womb You have been my God.
AMP.11:
Do not be far from me, for trouble is near;
And there is no one to help.
AMP.12:
Many [enemies like] bulls have surrounded me;
Strong bulls of Bashan have encircled me.
AMP.13:
They open wide their mouths against me,
Like a ravening and a roaring lion.
AMP.14:
I am poured out like water,
And all my bones are out of joint.
My heart is like wax;
It is melted [by anguish] within me.
AMP.15:
My strength is dried up like a fragment of clay pottery;
And my [dry] tongue clings to my jaws;
And You have laid me in the dust of death.
AMP.16:
For [a pack of] dogs have surrounded me;
A gang of evildoers has encircled me,
They pierced my hands and my feet.
AMP.17:
I can count all my bones;
They look, they stare at me.
AMP.18:
They divide my clothing among them
And cast lots for my garment.
AMP.19:
But You, O LORD, do not be far from me;
O You my help, come quickly to my assistance.
AMP.20:
Rescue my life from the sword,
My only life from the paw of the dog (the executioner).
AMP.21:
Save me from the lion's mouth;
From the horns of the wild oxen You answer me.
AMP.22:
I will tell of Your name to my countrymen;
In the midst of the congregation I will praise You.
AMP.23:
You who fear the LORD [with awe-inspired reverence], praise Him!
All you descendants of Jacob, honor Him.
Fear Him [with submissive wonder], all you descendants of Israel.
AMP.24:
For He has not despised nor detested the suffering of the afflicted;
Nor has He hidden His face from him;
But when he cried to Him for help, He listened.
AMP.25:
My praise will be of You in the great assembly.
I will pay my vows [made in the time of trouble] before those who [reverently] fear Him.
AMP.26:
The afflicted will eat and be satisfied;
Those who [diligently] seek Him and require Him [as their greatest need] will praise the LORD.
May your hearts live forever!
AMP.27:
All the ends of the earth will remember and turn to the LORD,
And all the families of the nations will bow down and worship before You,
AMP.28:
For the kingship and the kingdom are the Lord's
And He rules over the nations.
AMP.29:
All the prosperous of the earth will eat and worship;
All those who go down to the dust (the dead) will bow before Him,
Even he who cannot keep his soul alive.
AMP.30:
Posterity will serve Him;
They will tell of the Lord to the next generation.
AMP.31:
They will come and declare His righteousness
To a people yet to be born—that He has done it [and that it is finished].

MSG.1:
A David psalm
God, God... my God!
Why did you dump me
miles from nowhere?
MSG.2:
Doubled up with pain, I call to God
all the day long. No answer. Nothing.
I keep at it all night, tossing and turning.
MSG.3:
And you! Are you indifferent, above it all,
leaning back on the cushions of Israel's praise?
MSG.4: We know you were there for our parents:
MSG.5:
they cried for your help and you gave it;
they trusted and lived a good life.
MSG.6:
And here I am, a nothing—an earthworm,
something to step on, to squash.
MSG.7:
Everyone pokes fun at me;
they make faces at me, they shake their heads:
MSG.8:
"Let's see how God handles this one;
since God likes him so much, let him help him!"
MSG.9:
And to think you were midwife at my birth,
setting me at my mother's breasts!
MSG.10:
When I left the womb you cradled me;
since the moment of birth you've been my God.
MSG.11:
Then you moved far away
and trouble moved in next-door.
I need a neighbor.
MSG.12:
Herds of bulls come at me,
the raging bulls stampede,
MSG.13:
Horns lowered, nostrils flaring,
like a herd of buffalo on the move.
MSG.14:
I'm a bucket kicked over and spilled,
every joint in my body has been pulled apart.
My heart is a blob
of melted wax in my gut.
MSG.15:
I'm dry as a bone,
my tongue black and swollen.
They have laid me out for burial
in the dirt.
MSG.16:
Now packs of wild dogs come at me;
thugs gang up on me.
They pin me down hand and foot,
MSG.17:
and lock me in a cage—a bag
Of bones in a cage, stared at
by every passerby.
MSG.18:
They take my wallet and the shirt off my back,
and then throw dice for my clothes.
MSG.19:
You, God—don't put off my rescue!
Hurry and help me!
MSG.20:
Don't let them cut my throat;
don't let those mongrels devour me.
MSG.21:
If you don't show up soon,
I'm done for—gored by the bulls,
meat for the lions.
MSG.22:
Here's the story I'll tell my friends when they come to worship,
and punctuate it with Hallelujahs:
MSG.23:
Shout Hallelujah, you God-worshipers;
give glory, you sons of Jacob;
adore him, you daughters of Israel.
MSG.24:
He has never let you down,
never looked the other way
when you were being kicked around.
He has never wandered off to do his own thing;
he has been right there, listening.
MSG.25:
Here in this great gathering for worship
I have discovered this praise-life.
And I'll do what I promised right here
in front of the God-worshipers.
MSG.26:
Down-and-outers sit at God's table
and eat their fill.
Everyone on the hunt for God
is here, praising him.
"Live it up, from head to toe.
Don't ever quit!"
MSG.27:
From the four corners of the earth
people are coming to their senses,
are running back to God.
Long-lost families
are falling on their faces before him.
MSG.28:
God has taken charge;
from now on he has the last word.
MSG.29:
All the power-mongers are before him
—worshiping!
All the poor and powerless, too
—worshiping!
Along with those who never got it together
—worshiping!
MSG.30:
Our children and their children
will get in on this
As the word is passed along
from parent to child.
MSG.31:
Babies not yet conceived
will hear the good news—
that God does what he says.

NIV.1:
Psalm 22
For the director of music. To the tune of “The Doe of the Morning.” A psalm of David.
My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?
Why are you so far from saving me,
so far from the words of my groaning?
NIV.2:
O my God, I cry out by day, but you do not answer,
by night, and am not silent.
NIV.3:
Yet you are enthroned as the Holy One;
you are the praise of Israel.
NIV.4:
In you our fathers put their trust;
they trusted and you delivered them.
NIV.5:
They cried to you and were saved;
in you they trusted and were not disappointed.
NIV.6:
But I am a worm and not a man,
scorned by men and despised by the people.
NIV.7:
All who see me mock me;
they hurl insults, shaking their heads:
NIV.8:
“He trusts in the Lord;
let the Lord rescue him.
Let him deliver him,
since he delights in him.”
NIV.9:
Yet you brought me out of the womb;
you made me trust in you
even at my mother’s breast.
NIV.10:
From birth I was cast upon you;
from my mother’s womb you have been my God.
NIV.11:
Do not be far from me,
for trouble is near
and there is no one to help.
NIV.12:
Many bulls surround me;
strong bulls of Bashan encircle me.
NIV.13:
Roaring lions tearing their prey
open their mouths wide against me.
NIV.14:
I am poured out like water,
and all my bones are out of joint.
My heart has turned to wax;
it has melted away within me.
NIV.15:
My strength is dried up like a potsherd,
and my tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth;
you lay me in the dust of death.
NIV.16:
Dogs have surrounded me;
a band of evil men has encircled me,
they have pierced my hands and my feet.
NIV.17:
I can count all my bones;
people stare and gloat over me.
NIV.18:
They divide my garments among them
and cast lots for my clothing.
NIV.19:
But you, O Lord, be not far off;
O my Strength, come quickly to help me.
NIV.20:
Deliver my life from the sword,
my precious life from the power of the dogs.
NIV.21:
Rescue me from the mouth of the lions;
save me from the horns of the wild oxen.
NIV.22:
I will declare your name to my brothers;
in the congregation I will praise you.
NIV.23:
You who fear the Lord, praise him!
All you descendants of Jacob, honor him!
Revere him, all you descendants of Israel!
NIV.24:
For he has not despised or disdained
the suffering of the afflicted one;
he has not hidden his face from him
but has listened to his cry for help.
NIV.25:
From you comes the theme of my praise in the great assembly;
before those who fear you will I fulfill my vows.
NIV.26:
The poor will eat and be satisfied;
they who seek the Lord will praise him—
may your hearts live forever!
NIV.27:
All the ends of the earth
will remember and turn to the Lord,
and all the families of the nations
will bow down before him,
NIV.28:
for dominion belongs to the Lord
and he rules over the nations.
NIV.29:
All the rich of the earth will feast and worship;
all who go down to the dust will kneel before him—
those who cannot keep themselves alive.
NIV.30:
Posterity will serve him;
future generations will be told about the Lord.
NIV.31:
They will proclaim his righteousness
to a people yet unborn—
for he has done it.

NRSVCE.1:
My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?
Why are you so far from helping me, from the words of my groaning?
NRSVCE.2:
O my God, I cry by day, but you do not answer;
and by night, but find no rest.
NRSVCE.3:
Yet you are holy,
enthroned on the praises of Israel.
NRSVCE.4:
In you our ancestors trusted;
they trusted, and you delivered them.
NRSVCE.5:
To you they cried, and were saved;
in you they trusted, and were not put to shame.
NRSVCE.6:
But I am a worm, and not human;
scorned by others, and despised by the people.
NRSVCE.7:
All who see me mock at me;
they make mouths at me, they shake their heads;
NRSVCE.8:
“Commit your cause to the LORD; let him deliver —
let him rescue the one in whom he delights!”
NRSVCE.9:
Yet it was you who took me from the womb;
you kept me safe on my mother’s breast.
NRSVCE.10:
On you I was cast from my birth,
and since my mother bore me you have been my God.
NRSVCE.11:
Do not be far from me,
for trouble is near
and there is no one to help.
NRSVCE.12:
Many bulls encircle me,
strong bulls of Bashan surround me;
NRSVCE.13:
they open wide their mouths at me,
like a ravening and roaring lion.
NRSVCE.14:
I am poured out like water,
and all my bones are out of joint;
my heart is like wax;
it is melted within my breast;
NRSVCE.15:
my mouth is dried up like a potsherd,
and my tongue sticks to my jaws;
you lay me in the dust of death.
NRSVCE.16:
For dogs are all around me;
a company of evildoers encircles me.
My hands and feet have shriveled;
NRSVCE.17:
I can count all my bones.
They stare and gloat over me;
NRSVCE.18:
they divide my clothes among themselves,
and for my clothing they cast lots.
NRSVCE.19:
But you, O LORD, do not be far away!
O my help, come quickly to my aid!
NRSVCE.20:
Deliver my soul from the sword,
my life from the power of the dog!
NRSVCE.21:
Save me from the mouth of the lion!
From the horns of the wild oxen you have rescued me.
NRSVCE.22:
I will tell of your name to my brothers and sisters;
in the midst of the congregation I will praise you:
NRSVCE.23:
You who fear the LORD, praise him!
All you offspring of Jacob, glorify him;
stand in awe of him, all you offspring of Israel!
NRSVCE.24:
For he did not despise or abhor
the affliction of the afflicted;
he did not hide his face from me,
but heard when I cried to him.
NRSVCE.25:
From you comes my praise in the great congregation;
my vows I will pay before those who fear him.
NRSVCE.26:
The poor shall eat and be satisfied;
those who seek him shall praise the LORD.
May your hearts live forever!
NRSVCE.27:
All the ends of the earth shall remember
and turn to the LORD;
and all the families of the nations
shall worship before him.
NRSVCE.28:
For dominion belongs to the LORD,
and he rules over the nations.
NRSVCE.29:
To him, indeed, shall all who sleep in the earth bow down;
before him shall bow all who go down to the dust,
and I shall live for him.
NRSVCE.30:
Posterity will serve him;
future generations will be told about the Lord,
NRSVCE.31:
and proclaim his deliverance to a people yet unborn,
saying that he has done it.

NET.1: My God, my God, why have you abandoned me? I groan in prayer, but help seems far away.
NET.2: My God, I cry out during the day, but you do not answer, and during the night my prayers do not let up.
NET.3: You are holy; you sit as king receiving the praises of Israel.
NET.4: In you our ancestors trusted; they trusted in you and you rescued them.
NET.5: To you they cried out, and they were saved; in you they trusted and they were not disappointed.
NET.6: But I am a worm, not a man; people insult me and despise me.
NET.7: All who see me taunt me; they mock me and shake their heads.
NET.8: They say, “Commit yourself to the LORD! Let the LORD rescue him! Let the LORD deliver him, for he delights in him.”
NET.9: Yes, you are the one who brought me out from the womb and made me feel secure on my mother’s breasts.
NET.10: I have been dependent on you since birth; from the time I came out of my mother’s womb you have been my God.
NET.11: Do not remain far away from me, for trouble is near and I have no one to help me.
NET.12: Many bulls surround me; powerful bulls of Bashan hem me in.
NET.13: They open their mouths to devour me like a roaring lion that rips its prey.
NET.14: My strength drains away like water; all my bones are dislocated; my heart is like wax; it melts away inside me.
NET.15: The roof of my mouth is as dry as a piece of pottery; my tongue sticks to my gums. You set me in the dust of death.
NET.16: Yes, wild dogs surround me — a gang of evil men crowd around me; like a lion they pin my hands and feet.
NET.17: I can count all my bones; my enemies are gloating over me in triumph.
NET.18: They are dividing up my clothes among themselves; they are rolling dice for my garments.
NET.19: But you, O LORD, do not remain far away! You are my source of strength! Hurry and help me!
NET.20: Deliver me from the sword! Save my life from the claws of the wild dogs!
NET.21: Rescue me from the mouth of the lion, and from the horns of the wild oxen! You have answered me!
NET.22: I will declare your name to my countrymen! In the middle of the assembly I will praise you!
NET.23: You loyal followers of the LORD, praise him! All you descendants of Jacob, honor him! All you descendants of Israel, stand in awe of him!
NET.24: For he did not despise or detest the suffering of the oppressed; he did not ignore him; when he cried out to him, he responded.
NET.25: You are the reason I offer praise in the great assembly; I will fulfill my promises before the LORD’s loyal followers.
NET.26: Let the oppressed eat and be filled! Let those who seek his help praise the LORD! May you live forever!
NET.27: Let all the people of the earth acknowledge the LORD and turn to him! Let all the nations worship you!
NET.28: For the LORD is king and rules over the nations.
NET.29: All of the thriving people of the earth will join the celebration and worship; all those who are descending into the grave will bow before him, including those who cannot preserve their lives.
NET.30: A whole generation will serve him; they will tell the next generation about the sovereign Lord.
NET.31: They will come and tell about his saving deeds; they will tell a future generation what he has accomplished.
